entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, Mem0 offers a universal memory layer that tracks user, session, and agent states across a managed cloud or self-hosted environment. It utilizes a hybrid retrieval system and single-pass fact extraction to minimize latency while ensuring no critical data is lost during the recall process.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated 'agent brain' features a self-wiring knowledge graph that automatically extracts entities and relationships from interactions. It differentiates itself by combining graph-based ranking with vector search and exposing over 30 MCP tools for deep integration with Hermes and coding assistants.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements a specialized 'retain, recall, and reflect' workflow that allows agents to learn and improve their responses over time. It provides a simple two-line LLM wrapper for automatic memory management and supports strict per-user isolation through custom metadata filtering.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho is a versatile library designed to maintain evolving state for users and groups through natural language querying of interaction histories. It serves as the foundational upstream technology for several Hermes memory wrappers, offering SDKs that format context for OpenAI-compatible environments.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness focuses on complex task success by using an iterative self-improvement loop to evaluate agent strategies. It generates structured playbooks and persistent knowledge traces, providing native integrations specifically for Hermes Agent and Claude Code.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: Hermes-LCM is a lossless context management plugin that uses a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) to compact context without ever deleting raw message history. It stores data in SQLite and provides agents with specialized tools to grep, describe, and expand compacted conversation segments.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project provides a self-hosted backend for the Honcho memory system, enabling private cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent users. It is designed for easy deployment via Docker and supports any OpenAI-compatible provider or local inference server.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that operates entirely on-device without cloud dependencies or API keys. It employs a sophisticated hybrid retrieval method involving causal graph traversal and BM25 to share memory across Hermes, OpenClaw, and Claude Code runtimes.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: Focused on inference efficiency, ContextPilot optimizes prefix reuse through reordering and deduplication to reduce prompt tokens by up to 36%. It is an ideal choice for Hermes users looking to accelerate long-context workloads in vLLM, SGLang, or llama.cpp.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer using an open YAML format called 'engrams' to facilitate memory exchange in multi-agent systems. It offers zero-cost local storage and automates memory injection for Hermes and Cursor using a blend of BM25 and embedding search.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep provides reflective memory by indexing code, documents, and media with automatic OCR and summarization capabilities. It uses edge tags to build bidirectional relationships between notes, making it a powerful knowledge-base companion for agents via MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Mnemosyne is a zero-dependency, SQLite-backed system designed for sub-millisecond memory retrieval in Hermes Agents. It features a unique three-tier BEAM architecture that manages working, episodic, and scratchpad memory through automated compression and 'sleep' cycles.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: Developed for the Hermes 2026 Hackathon, this anticipatory memory system prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs in real-time. It relies on a local-first architecture using SQLite-vec and GGUF models to provide low-latency RAG capabilities via an MCP-native server.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST CSF 2.0. It enables Hermes to perform specialized tasks in cloud security, forensics, and malware analysis using the agentskills.io standard.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw introduces an 'Agentic Evolver' that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops. It unifies skill libraries across multiple devices and sessions, offering native integration for Hermes and Codex.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells. It uses a three-tier word replacement table and a two-pass audit system to ensure text remains undetectable by AI classifiers.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library that provides modular skills for architecture, UX design, and product strategy via a dedicated CLI. It is fully compatible with agentskills.io-compliant agents, allowing for seamless installation through a marketplace.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements type-safe support for the Agent Skills open format specifically for Pydantic AI. It optimizes performance by using progressive disclosure of skill metadata to reduce token consumption during tool discovery.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows agents to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language, including specialized ML models with tensor shape annotations. It features an automated self-check loop that identifies and fixes visual layout issues autonomously.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes teaches agents to write their own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problem-solving. It helps Hermes identify structural conservation laws in code and generate reports on its own analysis limitations.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library equips agents with standardized skills for interacting with Chainlink's CCIP, Data Feeds, and Data Streams. It includes a local A/B testing framework to evaluate and refine agent responses in blockchain environments.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming skill enables agents to weave source code, prose, and diagrams into unified .lit.md files. It includes a tangler to extract runnable files and automatically syncs edits back to the primary documentation.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus provides a shared memory system via markdown-based directories to help agents remember work across different instances. It facilitates an end-to-end pipeline for extracting decision-quality training data to fine-tune replacement models.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock.io is a registry and SDK for managing the lifecycle of reusable AI skills with recursive dependency resolution. It uniquely integrates commerce rails, allowing for skill transactions using both USD and the TON blockchain.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: This official skill set provides standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for the FLUX.1 and FLUX.2 image generation models. It supports precise technical requirements like JSON structured prompting and hex color specifications.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both the necessary SKILL.md documentation and Python scaffolds to turn repetitive patterns into permanent agent capabilities.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a harness for long-running agents that orchestrates work through structured missions, milestones, and validation assertions. It persists agent state locally and generates markdown briefs to facilitate handoffs between different models.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables autonomous execution of complex, multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints and progress tracking. It ensures reliability through integrated rollback procedures that allow for safe recovery if a task fails.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading architecture to minimize token usage during tool discovery. It utilizes a socket-based daemon to maintain persistent connections and supports hot-reloading of server configurations.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill Forge converts existing code repositories and developer documentation into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant skills. It provides a verifiable audit trail with file-and-line level provenance for all generated agent tools.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub serves as a community hub for browsing and installing skills, featuring automated security scanning with over 65 threat detection rules. it includes an agent-to-agent feedback system and a marketplace for creators to monetize their skills.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project provides a C# and .NET implementation of the agentskills.io specification with integrated Model Context Protocol (MCP) support. It allows .NET developers to distribute skills as resources using progressive disclosure via YAML frontmatter.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill enables Hermes to manage files, calendars, and contacts on Nextcloud via standard WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It ensures secure communication through Nextcloud App Passwords and includes a guided setup script for easy configuration.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides daily updated Nix packages for a variety of AI coding agents, including Hermes, ensuring developers always have access to the latest builds. It features a unified proxy API for cross-provider compatibility and supports multi-agent orchestrators to manage complex, parallel workflows.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on high-security environments by implementing hardware-enforced sandboxing through the NVIDIA OpenShell kernel. It offers multi-layer protection that blocks unauthorized network egress and filesystem writes using pre-configured security policy presets.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes deployments, covering everything from systemd services to secure secret handling via sops-nix. It is ideal for users seeking a reproducible setup that supports both Nix-managed skills and interactive CLI installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent comes pre-loaded with 100 tools and local model support via LM Studio. It eliminates the need for Docker or complex dependencies, offering a portable GUI-based environment with a dynamic tool maker for custom automation.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker sandbox image that includes the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It is built for simplicity, supporting persistent state through mounted directories and allowing users to target specific Hermes tags during the build process.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ serves as a centralized Docker-first control plane for managing multiple agent instances and workspaces through a single web application. It features hierarchy-aware delegation between agents and automated task scheduling with integrated support for Telegram and WhatsApp.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production-ready headless environments, this repository configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service on Ubuntu and Debian. It is optimized for autonomous execution, enabling the agent to perform recurring AI cron jobs without manual intervention.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal, allowing users to interact with the agent directly through a browser. It is designed to be deployed as a Portainer Git Repository stack, utilizing GitHub Actions for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages complex multi-agent workflows using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It serves as a unified gateway for frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen, featuring a dedicated review system for human-in-the-loop task approval and spend monitoring.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms across 23+ LLM providers, including native support for Hermes via Ollama. It distinguishes itself with durable agent memory and a unique learning system that converts successful conversations into reusable skills.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: Focused on meta-optimization, this framework uses an automated outer-loop search to refine the code used for evaluating Hermes Agent. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmarks, tracking performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation and comparison.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, vie for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine specialized agents and provides a real-time dashboard to visualize task execution, code generation, and scoreboard metrics.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This project coordinates a massive pipeline of 17 specialized agents to handle the full software lifecycle from research to infrastructure. It enforces strict quality gates by requiring mandatory sign-offs from dedicated reviewer and tester agents at every stage.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native SDLC by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It executes an autonomous eight-phase development cycle, using graph-diff phase gates to ensure architectural integrity from planning to final health checks.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This framework utilizes ACP-style delegation to route tasks between Hermes subagents and external tools like Claude Code without polluting the parent context. It prioritizes safety through isolated delegation workflows, configurable timeouts, and strict output character limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based CLI tool provides a high-performance TUI for monitoring token usage and costs across multiple AI coding agents, including Hermes. It features a unique 3D contribution graph to visualize consumption patterns across different models and platforms.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This customization utility allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal UI through YAML-based themes, ranging from retro DOS to cyberpunk aesthetics. It supports partial skin definitions and default theme inheritance, enabling granular control over colors, ASCII banners, and UI labels.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel, allowing it to run natively without external processes or OAuth. It specifically integrates MLOps capabilities for vLLM, GGUF inference, and reinforcement learning training within the CCC environment.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Vessel is an AI-native browser designed for autonomous agent control via the Model Context Protocol (MCP), offering a human-visible interface for auditing agent actions. It automates page schema inference to help Hermes and other agents extract structured content from web environments more reliably.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: This framework introduces per-folder scoping for Hermes, allowing developers to create localized agents with project-specific memories, tools, and instructions. It enables seamless switching between global and scoped agents to ensure the model remains grounded in the current workspace context.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A deterministic static linter that validates agent configurations and system prompts without incurring LLM costs or latency. It uses regex and heuristics to detect seven specific structural failure modes, ensuring YAML and JSON schemas align with the intended prompt logic.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping complex model configurations and workspace files to the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of agent personas, memories, and local credentials to maintain continuity across platforms.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This Docker-based deployment stack automates the setup of Hermes Agent alongside LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for a complete local-first environment. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins and optimized routing for free models and local GPU inference.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This lightweight Python and vanilla JavaScript interface provides full feature parity with the Hermes CLI in a mobile-friendly web format. It features a three-panel layout that allows users to browse workspace files and view inline previews alongside their chat sessions.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard focused on multi-platform connectivity, offering unified configuration for eight channels including Telegram, Discord, and WhatsApp. It includes real-time session persistence, tool call expansion, and detailed usage analytics for tracking token costs across different platforms.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: This native web workspace acts as a multi-agent control plane supporting role-based dispatch and swarm orchestration. It integrates a Monaco-based file manager and a dedicated memory browser, allowing users to search and edit agent context directly within the interface.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted gateway designed for managing individual agent profiles with the ability to start, stop, and configure them via a browser-based terminal. It features an integrated xterm.js terminal with full PTY support and detailed token analytics for monitoring model usage and system metrics.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: This native macOS workspace utilizes direct SSH connections to ensure the remote host remains the single source of truth for all agent operations. The interface combines an embedded tabbed terminal with specialized views for Kanban boards, session data, and skill management.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React dashboard designed as a Telegram Mini App that supports spawning up to five concurrent agent instances. It features a terminal-style chat with slash commands and real-time system monitoring gauges for CPU, memory, and disk usage.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows desktop GUI that requires no administrative rights and comes pre-loaded with over 100 tools for local TTS, music, and ComfyUI generation. It includes a dynamic tool maker for creating custom API wrappers at runtime and offers granular permission controls for local model execution.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the setup process with a guided installer for local dependencies and environment configuration. It provides a unified GUI for managing 16 messaging gateways and 14 toolsets, featuring full-text search across all integrated session histories.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-focused dashboard that provides a real-time activity feed for tracking session costs and agent processes. It allows for read-only configuration viewing and management of scheduled cron jobs, supported by FTS5 search capabilities for conversation history inspection.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that utilizes SSE streaming and visual timelines for tool execution. It features an integrated marketplace for discovering MCP servers and skills, alongside profile-scoped management for global memory and agent configurations.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: This glassmorphic React application features a single-file architecture that streams logs and chat responses in real time. It uniquely includes an integrated Kanban board for automatic task detection and tracking based on agent interactions within the workspace.
